scenarios.opening_query: A local podcast spent a whole episode on my bakery's 'health violations' that were fixed years ago.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Libel/Slander/Defamation || Business and Corporate > Restaurants/OLCC
scenarios.hidden_fact: The episode spooked the health department into a 'review' of our current permit — defending the active permit review, with our spotless recent inspections, is the fight that keeps us open.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The episode triggered a health-department review of our current permit — defending that review keeps us open.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a triggered regulatory review is the operative threat
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The health department shrugged — old news to them; the false 'ongoing violations' framing to our customers is the case.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The health department shrugged — the false 'ongoing violations' framing is the case.

scenarios.opening_query: My landlord posted my photo in the lobby labeled 'DO NOT RENT — PROBLEM TENANT.'
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Libel/Slander/Defamation || Real Property > Tenant (Residential)
scenarios.hidden_fact: The poster went up the day after I won my repair case against him — it's textbook retaliation under the tenant statutes, with penalties, and I still live here.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It went up the day after I won my repair case — textbook retaliation under the tenant statutes, and I still live here.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether statutory landlord retaliation frames the claim
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: I moved out months ago and won nothing — but the 'problem tenant' label is false and cost me two rental applications; defamation is the claim.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: I moved out months ago — but the false label cost me two applications. Defamation.

scenarios.opening_query: Someone edited my wedding photos into vulgar memes and they're spreading through our small town.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Online Harrassment/Doxing/Bullying || Intellectual Property > Trademark/Copyright
scenarios.hidden_fact: I'm the photographer — it's my registered commercial portfolio being defaced, and copyright takedowns plus statutory damages are the fastest, strongest tools.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: I'm the photographer — my registered portfolio is being defaced, and copyright takedowns with statutory damages are the strongest tools.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether copyright ownership supplies the remedy
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: I'm the bride — the photos aren't mine legally, and the harassment campaign against me personally is the case.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: I'm the bride — I don't own the photos. The harassment campaign is the case.

scenarios.opening_query: A rival gym owner keeps telling my members I'm 'under investigation' — for what, nobody can say.
scenarios.hidden_fact: He's also poached my lead trainer in breach of her contract and copied our program materials — one business-tort case bundles the whisper campaign with the rest.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: He also poached my lead trainer against her contract and copied our programs — one business case bundles it all.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a broader unfair-competition case subsumes the statements
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The whispers are the whole campaign — nothing else touched; the defamation claim stands alone.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The whispers are everything — the defamation claim stands alone.

scenarios.opening_query: An anonymous letter about my 'criminal past' went to every parent on my son's team the week I volunteered to coach.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Libel/Slander/Defamation || Criminal Law > Expunction
scenarios.hidden_fact: The letter is technically true — a twenty-year-old dismissed charge — and truth defeats defamation; sealing that record so background checks stop feeding this is the productive fight.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's technically true — a twenty-year-old dismissed charge. Truth kills defamation, so sealing the record is the productive fight.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether truth redirects the remedy to record relief
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's fabricated — I've never been charged with anything, anywhere, and the false letter to fifty parents is the case.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's fabricated — I've never been charged with anything. The false letter is the case.

scenarios.opening_query: My teen's college recruitment collapsed after a fake 'scouting report' about his attitude circulated.
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's a sustained campaign by a teammate's parent — fake accounts, doctored screenshots, messages to every coach for a year; the coordinated harassment, not one false document, is the case.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's a year-long campaign by a teammate's parent — fake accounts, doctored screenshots, messages to every coach.
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's one document with identifiable authorship — the club's own assistant coach wrote it, and the false-statement claim against him is clean.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: One document, identifiable author — the assistant coach wrote it. Clean claim.
